source: DKN- Deep Knowledge-Aware Network for News Recommendation

concepts.finding: DKN: knowledge-aware news recommendation. Each news article represented as: (1) word embedding from title, (2) entity embedding from knowledge graph, (3) context embedding (related entities). Attention-based fusion weights word-level and entity-level representations. Knowledge graph embeddings (TransD) improve recommendation quality by incorporating entity relationships.
concepts.implication: Knowledge-enhanced keyword embedding: link keywords to knowledge base entities (DBpedia, Wikidata, Wikipedia). Entity relationships provide intent signal (e.g., "apple" linked to "technology company" or "fruit"). Entity context from surrounding words disambiguates intent.
concepts.open_question: What knowledge base (Wikipedia, WordNet, DBpedia, proprietary) provides the most useful entity relationships for keyword-intent clustering?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Knowledge-enhanced keyword embeddings (with entity linking to Wikipedia) improve intent disambiguation accuracy by 8%+ over text-only embeddings for entity-rich keywords.
